sample_user_phrasings: what is the minimum attendance required
what happens if my attendance is low
minimum attendance to sit for exam
am I debarred for low attendance
answer_text: Attendance is calculated per course as (classes attended / classes held) x 100. The usual minimum is 75% per course to be eligible for the end-term exam. If you fall short for a genuine, documented reason (medical emergency, official college deputation, etc.), you can apply for condonation through the Academic Office before the end-term exam window opens. Falling below the cutoff without condonation typically means debarment from that course's end-term exam.
source: Academic Handbook, Attendance Norms section (approximated for this demo — confirm the exact %, course-wise exceptions, and condonation process with the Academic Office)

sample_user_phrasings: steps to withdraw from a subject
last date to drop a course
how to withdraw from a subject
remove a course from my registration
answer_text: Courses can be dropped without penalty only within the add/drop window, usually the first 1-2 weeks of the term, via the course registration module on the ERP. After that window closes, dropping a course needs written approval from your Area Chair or Program Chair, and it will usually show as a 'W' (Withdrawn) on your transcript instead of being removed entirely. Dropping after the mid-term is generally not permitted.
source: Academic Handbook, Course Registration & Add/Drop Policy (approximated — verify current-term deadlines on the ERP academic calendar)

answer_text: Apply through the 'Certificate Request' module on the ERP (or a written request to the Academic Office if the module isn't available to you), stating the purpose — bank loan, visa, internship, insurance, etc. Processing usually takes 3-5 working days. You can collect a physical copy from the Academic Office or request a digitally signed copy if your use case accepts one.
source: Student Services Handbook (approximated — confirm current turnaround time and digital-copy availability with Academic Office)

sample_user_phrasings: where do I complain about fee issues
how to get a fee refund
answer_text: First check your payment gateway receipt/transaction ID against what's shown on the ERP — most mismatches are a sync delay and resolve within 24-48 hours. If it still doesn't reflect, or you were charged incorrectly, email the Finance & Accounts Office with your registration number, term, and a description of the issue (attach the receipt). Refunds, where applicable, are typically processed within 2-3 weeks of approval per the Fee Refund Policy.
source: Finance Office / Fee Policy Circular (approximated — confirm current refund timelines with Accounts)

sample_user_phrasings: what is my grade point average
answer_text: CGPA = sum(credits of course x grade point of course) / sum(credits), across all completed terms. SGPA uses the same formula but restricted to a single term. Grade points follow the scale defined in the Academic Handbook's grading table (commonly a 0-10 or letter-grade scale mapped to grade points). Your live, personalized CGPA is on the ERP under Grades/Transcript — this bot only explains the formula, not your actual number.
source: Academic Handbook, Grading & CGPA Policy (approximated — confirm the exact grade-point scale table)

answer_text: Grade queries must be raised within a fixed window after grades are released — commonly 3-5 working days. Start by raising it directly with the course instructor via the ERP grade-query form or email. If unresolved, escalate in writing to the Area Chair or the Grade Grievance Committee. Note: policy usually allows you to view/discuss your answer script with the instructor, not necessarily a full external re-evaluation.
source: Academic Handbook, Grade Grievance Procedure (approximated — confirm the exact query window for the current term)
fallback_contact: Course instructor first, then Academic Office
